functions.name: asysocks.common.aiowrappers.readexactly_or_exc
documentation:
Helper function to read exactly N amount of data from the wire.
\:param reader: The reader object
\:type reader: asyncio.StreamReader
\:param n: The maximum amount of bytes to read.
\:type n: int
\:param timeout: Time in seconds to wait for the reader to return data
\:type timeout: int
\:return: bytearray

functions.name: asysocks.common.aiowrappers.read_or_exc
documentation:
Helper function to read N amount of data from the wire.
\:param reader: The reader object
\:type reader: asyncio.StreamReader
\:param n: The maximum amount of bytes to read. BEWARE: this only sets an upper limit of the data to be read
\:type n: int
\:param timeout: Time in seconds to wait for the reader to return data
\:type timeout: int
\:return: bytearray

functions.name: asysocks.common.aiowrappers.readuntil_or_exc
documentation:
Helper function to read stream until separator is hit.
\:param reader: The reader object
\:type reader: asyncio.StreamReader
\:param n: The maximum amount of bytes to read. BEWARE: this only sets an upper limit of the data to be read
\:type n: int
\:param timeout: Time in seconds to wait for the reader to return data
\:type timeout: int
\:return: bytearray

classes.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.get_extra_info
documentation: Get optional transport information.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.is_closing
documentation: Return True if the transport is closing or closed.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.close
documentation:
Close the transport.
Buffered data will be flushed asynchronously.  No more data
will be received.  After all buffered data is flushed, the
protocol's connection_lost() method will (eventually) be
called with None as its argument.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.set_protocol
documentation: Set a new protocol.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.get_protocol
documentation: Return the current protocol.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.is_reading
documentation: Return True if the transport is receiving.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.pause_reading
documentation:
Pause the receiving end.
No data will be passed to the protocol's data_received()
method until resume_reading() is called.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.resume_reading
documentation:
Resume the receiving end.
Data received will once again be passed to the protocol's
data_received() method.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.set_write_buffer_limits
documentation:
Set the high- and low-water limits for write flow control.
These two values control when to call the protocol's
pause_writing() and resume_writing() methods.  If specified,
the low-water limit must be less than or equal to the
high-water limit.  Neither value can be negative.
The defaults are implementation-specific.  If only the
high-water limit is given, the low-water limit defaults to an
implementation-specific value less than or equal to the
high-water limit.  Setting high to zero forces low to zero as
well, and causes pause_writing() to be called whenever the
buffer becomes non-empty.  Setting low to zero causes
resume_writing() to be called only once the buffer is empty.
Use of zero for either limit is generally sub-optimal as it
reduces opportunities for doing I/O and computation
concurrently.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.get_write_buffer_size
documentation: Return the current size of the write buffer.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.get_write_buffer_limits
documentation:
Get the high and low watermarks for write flow control.
Return a tuple (low, high) where low and high are
positive number of bytes.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.write
documentation:
Write some data bytes to the transport.
This does not block; it buffers the data and arranges for it
to be sent out asynchronously.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.writelines
documentation:
Write a list (or any iterable) of data bytes to the transport.
The default implementation concatenates the arguments and
calls write() on the result.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.write_eof
documentation:
Close the write end after flushing buffered data.
(This is like typing ^D into a UNIX program reading from stdin.)
Data may still be received.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.can_write_eof
documentation: Return True if this transport supports write_eof(), False if not.

methods.name: asysocks.unicomm.common.transport.UNITransport.abort
documentation:
Close the transport immediately.
Buffered data will be lost.  No more data will be received.
The protocol's connection_lost() method will (eventually) be
called with None as its argument.
Base class for WSNET transport.
